rationale: Recent territorial gains provide tactical momentum; a precision strike against Tethys’ mobilization centers will exploit their vulnerability following battlefield losses and deter reinforcement.
Joint naval exercises by Tethys and allies signal maritime escalation risk, requiring a visible counter-demonstration to assert naval presence and resolve.
Sustain pressure along the forward line of contact to disrupt enemy regrouping and protect newly seized positions amid ongoing information warfare targeting troop morale.
With crisis at 7.0 and Tethys receiving advanced weapons, offensive cyber operations can degrade command-and-control systems before new coalition-supplied assets are fully integrated.
Counter Tethys’ propaganda offensive by amplifying internal ethnic and political divisions through tailored disinformation, weakening domestic support for continued resistance.
Target critical nodes in Tethys-controlled pipeline infrastructure to reduce Novaris’ exposure to energy blackmail and retaliate for economic leverage abuse.
With only 12% sanctions severity and weak international support (30%), prioritize securing alternative export routes and domestic energy diversification to reduce dependence on Tethys pipelines.
Anticipate potential pipeline disruptions by stockpiling critical energy reserves, insulating the economy from sudden supply shocks amid rising crisis levels.
Direct targeted funding to accelerate non-Tethys energy infrastructure development, reducing long-term strategic vulnerability without escalating sanctions that lack coalition backing.
Given the narrow military balance and recent territorial losses, a precision strike on Novaris mobilization centers will disrupt their offensive momentum and exploit their overextended logistics.
Conducting a visible naval demonstration reinforces our defensive posture and signals resolve to both the adversary and our allies amid rising crisis levels.
Increase frontline surveillance to detect and counter potential follow-on offensives after Novaris’s recent battlefield gains.

With crisis at 7.0 and confirmed strain on Novaris logistics, a targeted cyber operation against their military command infrastructure can degrade coordination and capitalize on battlefield overextension.
Undermine domestic support in Novaris by amplifying narratives of military failure and corruption, exploiting their ongoing propaganda offensive and ethnic tensions within Tethyan populations.
Disrupt critical energy export nodes to inflict economic pain, leveraging Tethys’s control over pipeline routes and Novaris’s revenue vulnerability.
With international support at only 30% and a key ally facing domestic pressure, urgent bilateral outreach is essential to preserve coalition cohesion and prevent diplomatic unraveling.
Establishing protected corridors improves our moral standing internationally and creates leverage in future negotiations, especially as territory loss and crisis severity mount.
Launch a global information campaign highlighting Novaris aggression and Tethys’s democratic legitimacy to boost international support beyond its current weak level.
With sanctions at 12% and economy under strain, securing critical supplies now is essential to sustain war efforts and insulate against future disruptions or escalation in economic warfare.
Pursue immediate energy and arms procurement deals with key neutral or supportive states to offset limited domestic production and maintain military readiness.
Leverage control of transiting energy pipelines to restrict Novaris’s export capacity, directly targeting their 30% revenue dependency and compounding their economic vulnerability.
